text:
The HHAs are required by regulations to have written policies concerning the acceptance 
of patients by the agency. These include consideration of the physical facilities available 
in the patient's place of residence, the homebound status, and the attitudes of family 
members for the purpose of evaluating the feasibility of meeting the patient's medical 
needs in the home health setting. When personnel of the agency make such an initial 
evaluation visit, the cost of the visit is considered an
